steps.title: Identify the structural difference between parasite types
steps.content:
Three-host parasites like Anisakis sp. depend on at least two vertebrate species and showed declining abundance as temperatures rose.

Two-host parasites like C. kanabus depend on only one vertebrate species and remained stable.

The passage emphasizes that vertebrates are climate-vulnerable, raising the key question: what does this contrasting pattern suggest?

steps.title: Apply the structural difference to find the correct inference
steps.content:
Choice B captures the logical inference: warming-induced population changes among Puget Sound vertebrates may have suppressed some parasite populations, especially three-host parasites that depend on at least two vertebrate species, while no changes among the eight studied host species affected two-host parasite abundance.

The other three either reverse the relationship, deny that warming affected populations, or claim more host dependencies reduce sensitivity to warming.

That is the correct answer.
